---
title: "Reproducing the SONG Paper Figures with songR"
subtitle: "Key figures from Senanayake et al. (2021, IEEE TNNLS), reproduced with the songR R/C++ package"
author: "R. Heller"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    embed-resources: true
    fig-align: center
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    geometry: margin=2.5cm
    fig-align: center
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
here <- function(...) file.path("tests/paper-figures", ...)
if (!file.exists(here("results.csv"))) here <- function(...) file.path(".", ...)
res <- read.csv(here("results.csv"))
```

## About

This report reproduces the central figures of the original SONG paper —

> Senanayake, D. A., Wang, W., Naik, S. H., & Halgamuge, S. (2021).
> Self-Organizing Nebulous Growths for Robust and Incremental Data
> Visualization. *IEEE TNNLS*, 32(10), 4588–4602.
> doi:10.1109/TNNLS.2020.3023941

— using **songR**, the SONG-inspired R/C++ implementation, on the same
datasets and the paper's Table I hyperparameters (`epsilon = 0.99`,
`epochs = 100`, `a = 1.577`, `b = 0.895`) with the default UMAP dispersion.
MNIST and Fashion-MNIST are subsampled for tractable runtime; COIL-20 is run
in full. Cluster quality is reported as Adjusted Mutual Information (AMI;
k-means on the embedding vs. ground-truth labels, mean over 5 seeds).

These figures show that **songR reproduces the qualitative structure the paper
reports** — separated digit/garment clusters, COIL-20's circular rotation
manifolds, clean recovery of many Gaussian blobs, and stable incremental
growth. For the quantitative *songR vs. original-Python* parity (AMI equality,
Procrustes overlays), see `tests/reproduction-report/`.

```{r}
r <- res; names(r) <- c("Figure", "Dataset", "n", "Clusters", "AMI (songR)")
r[["AMI (songR)"]] <- round(r[["AMI (songR)"]], 3)
knitr::kable(r, caption = "songR reproductions of the paper datasets.")
```

## Fig. 4 — MNIST

Handwritten digits (PCA→20). SONG separates the ten digit classes into
distinct islands, as in the paper.

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(here("figures", "fig4_mnist.png"))
```

## Fig. 3 — Fashion-MNIST

Garment images (PCA→20). The footwear and clothing super-groups separate, with
finer structure within each — matching the paper's harder-than-MNIST result.

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(here("figures", "fig3_fashion_mnist.png"))
```

## Fig. 8 — COIL-20 topology

20 objects photographed over 72 rotation angles (PCA→300). SONG preserves the
rotational topology, so each object forms a **closed loop / circular cluster** —
the paper's signature topology-preservation result.

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(here("figures", "fig8_coil20.png"))
```

## Fig. 7 — Gaussian blobs

A many-cluster, high-dimensional stress test. SONG cleanly recovers all
clusters (high AMI), illustrating the paper's noise/robustness claims.

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(here("figures", "fig7_blobs.png"))
```

## Figs. 3–4 — Heterogeneous incremental updates

SONG's signature capability: train on a subset of classes, then add **new,
previously unseen** classes without reinitializing. Here we fit MNIST digits
0–4, then incrementally add 5–9 via `update()`. The original five classes keep
their positions while the new ones grow into place.

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(here("figures", "fig3_4_incremental.png"))
```

## Reproduce

```bash
Rscript tests/paper-figures/generate_paper_figures.R   # figures + results.csv
quarto render tests/paper-figures/paper_figures.qmd     # html + pdf
```

The real datasets are PCA-reduced per the paper protocol and kept out of the
repository (`.housekeeping/`); the full-scale tutorial scripts in `tutorials/`
reproduce every paper figure and table on the complete datasets.
